name: Srinivas Komma
text:
The Ferst Center for the Arts is a fantastic venue with excellent acoustics, comfortable seating, and a welcoming atmosphere.

The venue hosts a great variety of performances, from concerts to theater productions, all with top-notch production quality. The location on Georgia Tech’s campus is convenient, with nearby parking options.

Overall, it’s a great place to experience live performances in Atlanta. I highly recommend it!

text:
I've been to the Ferst Center for several events.
It is nice, clean, and comfortable, and the views are great.
The biggest obstacle is parking.
Skip the closer outdoor ground lot and opt for the parking garage instead. The outdoor lot is more convenient, but there are ALWAYS issues in paying to park (online, app, and manually). Evertime we have used the lot we have encountered issues with paying. Avoid it at all costs.

Ferst Center For The Arts
Awesome auditorium for professional arts performances in the heart of Atlanta. Located in Georgia Tech campus beside I-75 and I-85 in Midtown. Nice facility suitable for most concerts. responseFromOwnerText:
We're sorry to hear that you had that experience, Ms. Williams. Some navigation devices direct customers to the parking lot on the back side of the theater, a much longer walk to the front door than from the Student Center Parking Deck and surface lot. We hope you visit us again, and when you do, our driving directions page https://arts.gatech.edu/driving-directions can guide you to the parking locations that are only 500 feet from the theater. 
Thank you for leaving your comment, and we're so glad that you enjoyed the show!

name: Sandra Hughes
text: The Ferst  Center for the Arts at Georgia Tech in Atlanta is an amazing venue. There is not a bad view in the 900-seat house and the acoustics are wonderful. I performed there recently for Georgia Tech's annual Transformative Narratives Story Crawl. I was honored to be one of the headliners at the 3 Day, 7 event story festival. In the Spirit , a story and song duo from Chicago and New York city-based multi award-winning storyteller Jamie Brickhouse headlined as well. There were also seven outstanding local storytellers on the program. Whatever your taste - music, dance or drama - the F e r s t Center for the Arts is sure to please. The parking lot and parking decks are available a short walk from the venue. There is easy access to the location from North Avenue via Cherry Street. Check out the the Georgia Tech Story Crawl and plan to attend next time. There are workshops with Master coaches as well as open mic opportunities with great hospitality and comradery all round.

name: Lorianna Ayson
text: Perfect venue for the dance contest we attended. Comfortable lobby seats for the dance families, bathrooms could have been serviced a little more often, stadium style seating in auditorium had perfect views to the stage area from each seat (though opera glasses or binoculars help if you're toward the back). My only grief was that if you are there in a Sunday ... all day... for a contest or like production, there is NO FOOD to be found without at least a 3/3 mile walk. The Ga Tech student center shares the courtyard area but the food putlets are closed on sundays.

title: Ferst Center For The Arts

name: HP Jacket
text:
This is a great venue for smaller productions.  Recently, parking lot access at the adjacent lot/deck has under-performed and Georgia Tech should address this because it is an unreasonable and unpleasant burden.  Recent performance of C.S. Lewis "Most Reluctant Convert" had adjacent streets gridlocked because of extremely slow parking lot admittance.
For this reason show up early to shows.  The theater itself warrants 5 stars.  Cozy, no bad seats and acoustics are better than most Atlanta venues (which isn't saying much, lol).
Music, dance, theater, poetry and stage artists are well presented at the Ferst Center.  Highly recommend.
Over the years we have enjoyed seeing the following acts at the Ferst Center: BBVD (Big Bad VooDoo Daddy), the Munich Symphony Orchestra, Jake Shimabukuro, Penn & Teller, Flying Karamazov Bros., Bill Cosby, Pilobus, Paula Poundstone, and others...
